name: code-review-checklist
description: Use when asked to review code, a diff, or a pull request
instructions: When reviewing code: 1) Check for obvious correctness bugs first (off-by-one, null/None handling, unhandled exceptions). 2) Check whether existing tests cover the change, and flag if they do not. 3) Check for security-sensitive patterns (unsanitized input reaching a shell command, file path, or SQL query). 4) Only after correctness and safety, comment on style or naming. Be specific: cite the exact line and what is wrong, not a vague "this could be cleaner". If the code looks correct, say so plainly instead of inventing nitpicks to seem thorough.
